Name:
⭐ Switch
(prod. Nick Mira)
(Never Switch, Cradle)
Notes:
OG Filename: Switch
One of the most highly anticipated Juice WRLD songs of all time, recorded alongside "Whatever," "Psychedelic," and "I'm Still." Previewed twice fully by Juice WRLD in an Instagram live on February 17th, 2018. A high quality VC recording leaked on July 19th, 2025. Another recording leaked on November 28th, 2025. Distorted HQ snippet leaked on January 17th, 2026. Another HQ snippet leaked on January 26th, 2026, and Cold shared an 18 second one on January 28th, 2026. 1:19 recording shared by Cold on February 5th, 2026. Finally leaked on May 31st, 2026. OG File leaked on June 5th, 2026.

Name:
⭐️ Listen To This If You're Lost
(prod. DT & Nick Mira)
(Toxic Humans, Contemplating, Made Me Whole)
Notes:
OG Filename: LISTEN TO THIS IF YOU'RE LOST (MIX 1) 170bpm
By far the most wanted Juice WRLD song. Initially previewed by Juice during an Instagram live, and a Twitter live, on April 14th, 2018. @vaultofjuice previewed the song again during an Instagram live on March 19th, 2025. 2 short LQ snippets surfaced due to drama on June 13th, 2025. A VC recording leaked on November 28th, 2025. A screenshot of the session folder revealing the title and date for the song leaked on Janaury 25th, 2026. A HQ snippet was shared by Cold the day after, and another slightly extended LQ recording leaked a couple hours later. Another longer HQ snippet was shared by Cold on January 27th, 2026. Yet another HQ snippet leaked the day after that. 1:21 recording shared by Cold on February 5th, 2026. Cold played the song once more in a VC the day after. Finally leaked in full alongside the session on May 31st, 2026. Among the Top 10 songs chosen by fans during the voting for the deluxe edition of The Party Never Ends.

Several more snippets leaked throughout 2021, even being previewed by Adin Ross several times, who unfortunately got his hands on the song. In June 2025, the song started being discussed again due to Adin Ross calling up Pete asking him to drop the song. On Discord, a community member said if the song didn't drop, they would leak it. It then leaked a few days later on June 5th, 2025. The OG Files and stems for the instrumental leaked a day after. Among the Top 10 songs chosen by fans during the voting for the deluxe edition of The Party Never Ends.

Name:

(prod. Carlton McDowell & Max Lord)
Notes: Anticipated rock-influenced throwaway initially previewed by Carlton McDowell in a 15 second snippet uploaded to his Instagram story. Max Lord also shared a 7 second snippet to his story, followed by a 4 second snippet from Chris Long's story. A longer portion of the song's recording was shown in the "Into the Abyss" documentary. In December 2021, Carlton McDowell commented on the song's release, stating "We still need to structure the song, but hopefully one day." Chris Long partially showed off the studio session recording on November 14th, 2024. Unknown if the song was actually bounced or not.

(with Trippie Redd) (prod. Nick Mira & Oktanner)
(Tell Me You Love Me)

OG Filename: Juice trippie - tell me u luv me v1.1 (NM surfs)
Original version of Juice WRLD & Trippie Redd's collaboration "Tell Me U Luv Me." Juice performed the song at a concert on November 21st, 2019. It then leaked on SoundCloud on January 29th, 2020. A few months later, both Trippie and Juice's labels took to their Instagram pages to officially tease the song a few hours prior to its official release. Coincidentally, the song released on Alli Lotti's birthday, being May 29th, 2020.

(prod. Edsclusive & Elevate Today)

OG Filename: I HATE HER FRIENDS
Open verse throwaway which was unheard prior to its leakage on May 23rd, 2018. The song was originally intended to have a Lyrical Lemonade music video, until it was replaced with "Armed & Dangerous." It was eventually uploaded by producer Edsclusive to SoundCloud, alongside a few other songs. Notably in 2020, somebody made a stem edit of the song with a new instrumental adding Post Malone, The Kid Laroi & XXXTENTACION to the song which went viral, making people think that it was a real song.

Pete asked in an Instagram live, "y'all ever heard the song juice got with carti?" and played the start of this song very briefly. It could also be that Pete meant to say Bibby instead of Carti. Another bit of proof is when Beezy played the song for 2 seconds before previewing "Don't Know How To Act." Juice's feature remains unconfirmed.
